hypothesis: In a multi-fidelity MADE setting with MLIP pre-evaluation and a DFT oracle, an agent that models cross-fidelity uncertainty via Gaussian-process co-kriging and selects candidates by maximizing expected convex-hull improvement at the DFT level will discover at least 50% more DFT-confirmed, Materials Project–novel stable compounds than a MatterGen-plus-diversity baseline within 50 DFT evaluations on held-out ternary systems.
